title: Avoid equality operators in for loop termination
description: Equality operators (==, !=) should not be used in the termination condition of for loops. Using equality checks can lead to infinite loops or off-by-one errors, making code less reliable and harder to maintain. Prefer using relational operators to ensure correct loop execution and improve code clarity.
recommendation: Use relational operators (<, >, <=, >=) in for loop termination conditions instead of equality operators.

checks.message: Use relational operators (<, >, <=, >=) in for loop termination conditions instead of equality operators.
